summary: Executed comprehensive backend tests for CivicReel FastAPI. 21/21 pytest tests passed (100%). Covered: health, categories (10 keys), auth negative (invalid session_id/missing header/invalid bearer -> 401), auth/me happy path with seeded MongoDB session, profile update, username uniqueness conflict (409), post CRUD flow, feed with author enrichment, like toggle on/off, comments add+list, category+text search, user search, user profile with post_count, user posts, and logout invalidating session. Verified no '_id' leaks in any response and datetime handling uses tz-aware datetimes (ensure_aware helper in server.py). No backend issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 357-line file which is acceptable for MVP size but should be modularized (routers/auth, routers/posts, routers/users) as it grows past ~700 lines.
auth/logout returns {'ok': True} even without a bearer token (silently no-ops). Consider returning 401 for missing token for consistency, though current behavior is safe.
POST /api/posts requires user.onboarded=True. Frontend must enforce onboarding flow before allowing post creation, else users get 400.

search_posts and users/search accept empty q — users/search returns [] for empty q (good), but posts/search with empty q+empty category returns full feed which may be intentional; consider capping to avoid heavy queries.
No pagination on comments/list or user posts (hard-capped at 200/100). Fine for MVP.
Consider adding input length validation on description and slogan (Pydantic max_length) to prevent abusive payloads.
seed_data_creation: Seeded users (user_test_1 as tester1 India/Karnataka onboarded=true; user_test_2 as otheruser for uniqueness test) and user_sessions (session_token=test_token_abc, 7-day expiry, tz-aware) via pymongo in conftest fixture. Auto-teardown at session end cleans users/sessions/posts/likes/comments.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. When retesting, run: `cd /app/backend/tests && python -m pytest test_civicreel_api.py -v`. Seed fixture is autouse=session-scope so no manual DB setup needed. BASE_URL is read from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env. For frontend testing, use mobile viewport 375x667 and note that Google OAuth cannot be simulated in browser automation — you'll need to seed a session (like conftest does) and inject session_token into AsyncStorage/localStorage as expo-router auth store expects.
